records.ety: [OE. magestee, F. majest, L. majestas, fr. an old compar. of magnus great. See Major, Master.]

defs: Hence, used with the possessive pronoun, the title of an emperor, king or queen; -- in this sense taking a plural; as, their majesties attended the concert.
quotes: In all the public writs which he [Emperor Charles V.] now issued as King of Spain, he assumed the title of Majesty, and required it from his subjects as a mark of respect. Before that time all the monarchs of Europe were satisfied with the appellation of Highness or Grace.

defs: Dignity; elevation of manner or style.
